raw_consumer_statement:
This is my third issue with Citi. 
I have a feeling they are using AI to process disputes. 

This is a dispute with XXXX for three bottles of sea buckthorn oil. 
The package was received damaged and returned. I assumed the refund was processed but later found out from XXXX that they are claiming there was an error or issue refunding me back to my Citi bank card. They emailed me multple times explaining this that even though they owe me the refund and have tried to refund me, it didn't go through. They told me to take this to my bank and they can reopen and win the dispute. 

I have sent this, word for word, to Citi bank several times. It makes no sense with such cut and dry support that this would be denied. My only thought is they are not using humans to investigate these disputes but rather using pure AI. 

XXXX  XXXX on XX/XX/year>
